channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden toilet was stolen from English palace in 'audacious raid,' prosecutor says
item.description: It was not your typical smash-and-grab burglary and the booty was precious: a toilet worth more than its weight in gold.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Iran can manufacture 6 nuclear weapons with newly enriched uranium, UN Atomic agency report says
item.description: A report by the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) cautioned that the Islamic Republic of Iran is producing a large amount of uranium that could be used in six atomic bombs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: ‘Concession Agreement’ with Venezuela will be terminated
item.description: President Trump said the Biden-era “Concession Agreement” with Venezuela will be terminated as the country takes too long to take back its deportees.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy is coming to the White House to sign US-Ukraine critical minerals deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Wednesday that Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy will visit the White House on Friday to sign a long-sought minerals deal that will closely tie the two countries together for years to come.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, HANNA ARHIROVA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: High-level EU-US diplomatic talks are called off as transatlantic tensions rise
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned meeting between European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Secretary of State Marco Rubio was abruptly canceled Wednesday due to “scheduling issues,” coming as political tensions have increased between Europe and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bosnian Serb leader Dodik is convicted and sentenced for separatist activities as tensions surge
item.description: SARAJEVO, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — A court in Bosnia on Wednesday sentenced the pro-Russia Bosnian Serb president, Milorad Dodik, to one year in prison and banned him from politics for six years over his separatist actions as tensions mount in the fragile Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: AMER COHADZIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian prosecutors launch investigation into far-right populist who led canceled presidential vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian prosecutors launched a criminal investigation on Wednesday against a far-right populist who won the first round in a canceled presidential election last year, accusing him of election campaign funding abuses, supporting fascist groups and other offenses.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sees 'a thirst' for his 'gold card' visa idea with $5 million potential path to US citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump says he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat And Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: More than 100 firefighters battle blazes on South Africa's Table Mountain in Cape Town
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — More than 100 firefighters battled two blazes on the slopes of South Africa's Table Mountain on Wednesday, and national parks authorities said they were starting to bring them under control after the fires threatened to move toward nearby Cape Town.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Romanian far-right presidential frontrunner taken into custody after JD Vance's rebuke of European elections
item.description: Romanian candidate Calin Georgescu is being questioned by prosecutors after Vice President JD Vance criticized a "Soviet-era" shift in European democracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy looking for ‘NATO or something similar’ as he prepares for meeting with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy pushes for NATO membership or "something similar" ahead of expected meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrat begins CNN interview knocking 'nonsense' Trump-Ukraine report
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy criticized CNN's Nick Paton Walsh for suggesting that President Trump's intentions on Ukraine are unclear, while Trump's administration has been pushing negotiations directly with Russian officials and excluding Ukrainian leaders.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Nurse who said she wouldn't treat Israelis is charged with making threats
item.description: Sarah Abu Lebdeh, 26, who allegedly said she wouldn't treat Israeli medical patients, was charged with multiple federal offenses in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 7,000 workers from scam centers in Myanmar are awaiting repatriation after a regional crackdown
item.description: A new crackdown on online scam centers has led to over 7,000 people from around the world being held in a Myanmar border town awaiting repatriation.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai And Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jil Sander collection creates light from darkness as designers Lucie and Luke Meier leave the brand
item.description: The latest Jil Sander collection with shimmering surfaces cut dramatically against the darkened runway on the second day of Milan Fashion Week runway previews for next fall and winter.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Barry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff may travel to Middle East Sunday for Israel-Hamas ceasefire talks
item.description: Steve Witkoff, President Trump’s special envoy for the Middle East, may travel to the region on Sunday if indirect talks between Israel and Hamas on a second phase of a ceasefire deal show progress this week.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas agrees to return bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners
item.description: Hamas has agreed to return the bodies of more dead Israeli hostages with just days to go before the end of the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows further slight improvement as he battles pneumonia, the Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis has shown further slight improvement as he battles double pneumonia, but doctors still say his prognosis is still guarded.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield And Silvia Stellacci, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stress and fear roil a tiny, rapidly militarizing Japanese island near Taiwan
item.description: YONAGUNI, Japan (AP) — This tiny island on Japan's western frontier has no chain convenience stores. Nature lovers can dive with hammerhead sharks and watch miniature horses graze on a hill. But the wooded mountain ranges now carry radar sites.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power outage leaves most of Chile in darkness for hours
item.description: SANTIAGO, Chile (AP) — Electricity providers across Chile scrambled to restore service Tuesday after a sweeping blackout plunged 90% of the country into darkness, stranding commuters, knocking cell service offline and paralyzing businesses and daily life.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE and MATIAS BASUALDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on new exchange, leaving fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials said Tuesday they have reached an agreement to exchange the bodies of dead hostages for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, keeping their fragile ceasefire intact for at least a few more days.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: GABORONE, Botswana (AP) — The Botswana government signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company on Tuesday after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SELLO MOTSETA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supporters of separatist Bosnian Serb president Dodik rally as he awaits verdict fueling tensions
item.description: BANJA LUKA, Bosnia-Herzegovina (AP) — Supporters of separatist Bosnian Serb President Milorad Dodik rallied on Tuesday as tensions soared on the eve of a scheduled court verdict that could order the pro-Russia Serb leader banned from politics or even sentenced to prison.
item.dc:creator: RADUL RADOVANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: Radio Begum launched on International Women’s Day in March 2021, five months before the Taliban seized power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation
